sentence_reviews.text: Predictive distributions and belief states express uncertainty rather than only point estimates.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML probabilistic prediction material; AIMA probabilistic reasoning and belief-state material.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited sources support distributions and belief states as representations of uncertainty. The contrast with point estimates is a concise synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs is to connect Probability theory, Statistics & experimental design to downstream capabilities including Contrastive learning, Generative modeling, Calibration, uncertainty & risk objectives.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML/AIMA probabilistic reasoning and statistical inference; VAE paper as an example of probabilistic generative modeling.
sentence_reviews.notes: The probability/statistics and generative-modeling links are supported. Contrastive learning and the exact downstream list are not directly established by the candidate references.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML includes contrastive/self-supervised learning context, but the packet source does not directly establish distributions and beliefs as its substrate.
sentence_reviews.notes: Contrastive objectives often involve probabilistic or distributional views, but the exact substrate claim is too broad for the cited evidence.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML generative modeling material; VAE and diffusion references define probabilistic generative models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Generative modeling commonly models data distributions; 'substrate for' is atlas phrasing.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Calibration, uncertainty & risk objectives
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML discusses predictive uncertainty/calibration-related probabilistic prediction; NIST AI RMF discusses risk framing.
sentence_reviews.notes: Calibration and risk objectives rely on uncertainty estimates, but the exact combined capability name is graph terminology.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory, Statistics & experimental design.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML is relevant to probabilistic representations but does not directly support the full generic limitation sentence.
sentence_reviews.notes: The limitation appears generated and is not directly supported for distributions, uncertainty, and beliefs by the packet sources.

sentence_reviews.text: Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Probability theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML and AIMA probabilistic reasoning chapters treat distributions and beliefs as probability-based constructs.
sentence_reviews.notes: Probability theory directly underlies distributions and belief-state uncertainty representations.

sentence_reviews.text: Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Statistics & experimental design.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML statistical learning and probabilistic prediction material.
sentence_reviews.notes: Statistical estimation and predictive uncertainty support this dependency, though experimental design is not directly shown by the packet source.

edge_reviews.notes: Distributions and belief states are probability-theoretic objects, so requires/core is directly supported.
Statistics is central to estimating and evaluating predictive distributions, but the edge is less exact than the probability dependency.
